heading: Renewable fuel infrastructure grants
label: 17052 Renewable fuel infrastructure grants
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.heading: Definition of renewable fuel blend
children.label: a Definition of renewable fuel blend
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “renewable fuel blend” means a gasoline blend that contains not less than 11 percent, and not more than 85 percent, renewable fuel or diesel fuel that contains at least 10 percent renewable fuel.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program for making grants for providing assistance to retail and wholesale motor fuel dealers or other entities for the installation, replacement, or conversion of motor fuel storage and dispensing infrastructure to be used exclusively to store and dispense renewable fuel blends.

children.label: A
children.text: consideration of the public demand for each renewable fuel blend in a particular geographic area based on State registration records showing the number of flexible-fuel vehicles;

children.label: B
children.text: consideration of the opportunity to create or expand corridors of renewable fuel blend stations along interstate or State highways;

children.label: C
children.text: consideration of the experience of each applicant with previous, similar projects;

children.label: D
children.text: consideration of population, number of flexible-fuel vehicles, number of retail fuel outlets, and saturation of flexible-fuel vehicles; and

children.label: i
children.text: are most likely to maximize displacement of petroleum consumption, measured as a total quantity and a percentage;

children.label: ii
children.text: are best able to incorporate existing infrastructure while maximizing, to the extent practicable, the use of renewable fuel blends; and

children.label: iii
children.text: demonstrate the greatest commitment on the part of the applicant to ensure funding for the proposed project and the greatest likelihood that the project will be maintained or expanded after Federal assistance under this subsection is completed.

children.label: A
children.text: 33 percent of the estimated cost of the installation, replacement, or conversion of motor fuel storage and dispensing infrastructure; or

children.label: B
children.text: $180,000 for a combination of equipment at any one retail outlet location.

children.heading: Operation of renewable fuel blend stations
children.label: 4 Operation of renewable fuel blend stations
children.text: The Secretary shall establish rules that set forth requirements for grant recipients under this section that include providing to the public the renewable fuel blends, establishing a marketing plan that informs consumers of the price and availability of the renewable fuel blends, clearly labeling the dispensers and related equipment, and providing periodic reports on the status of the renewable fuel blend sales, the type and amount of the renewable fuel blends dispensed at each location, and the average price of such fuel.

children.heading: Notification requirements
children.label: 5 Notification requirements
children.text: Not later than the date on which each renewable fuel blend station begins to offer renewable fuel blends to the public, the grant recipient that used grant funds to construct or upgrade such station shall notify the Secretary of such opening. The Secretary shall add each new renewable fuel blend station to the renewable fuel blend station locator on its Website when it receives notification under this subsection.

children.heading: Double counting
children.label: 6 Double counting
children.text: No person that receives a credit under may receive assistance under this section.section 30C of title 26

children.heading: Reservation of funds
children.label: 7 Reservation of funds
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve funds appropriated for the renewable fuel blends infrastructure development grant program for technical and marketing assistance described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Retail technical and marketing assistance
children.label: c Retail technical and marketing assistance

children.text: technical advice for compliance with applicable Federal and State environmental requirements;
help in identifying supply sources and securing long-term contracts; and
provision of public outreach, education, and labeling materials.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a competitive grant pilot program (referred to in this subsection as the “pilot program”), to be administered through the Vehicle Technology Deployment Program of the Department, to provide not more than 10 geographically-dispersed project grants to State governments, Indian tribal governments, local governments, metropolitan transportation authorities, or partnerships of those entities to carry out 1 or more projects for the purposes described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: installation of infrastructure and equipment necessary to ensure adequate distribution of renewable fuel blends within the corridor;

children.label: B
children.text: installation of infrastructure and equipment necessary to directly support vehicles powered by renewable fuel blends; and

children.label: C
children.text: operation and maintenance of infrastructure and equipment installed as part of a project funded by the grant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall issue requirements for use in applying for grants under the pilot program.December 19, 2007

children.label: aa
children.text: the head of a State, tribal, or local government or a metropolitan transportation authority, or any combination of those entities; and

children.label: bb
children.text: a registered participant in the Vehicle Technology Deployment Program of the Department; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a description of the project proposed in the application, including the ways in which the project meets the requirements of this subsection;

children.label: bb
children.text: an estimate of the degree of use of the project, including the estimated size of fleet of vehicles operated with renewable fuels blend available within the geographic region of the corridor, measured as a total quantity and a percentage;

children.label: cc
children.text: an estimate of the potential petroleum displaced as a result of the project (measured as a total quantity and a percentage), and a plan to collect and disseminate petroleum displacement and other relevant data relating to the project to be funded under the grant, over the expected life of the project;

children.label: dd
children.text: a description of the means by which the project will be sustainable without Federal assistance after the completion of the term of the grant;

children.label: ee
children.text: a complete description of the costs of the project, including acquisition, construction, operation, and maintenance costs over the expected life of the project; and

children.label: ff
children.text: a description of which costs of the project will be supported by Federal assistance under this subsection.

children.heading: Partners
children.label: B Partners
children.text: An applicant under subparagraph (A) may carry out a project under the pilot program in partnership with public and private entities.

children.label: A
children.text: consider the experience of each applicant with previous, similar projects; and

children.label: i
children.text: are most likely to maximize displacement of petroleum consumption, measured as a total quantity and a percentage;

children.label: ii
children.text: are best able to incorporate existing infrastructure while maximizing, to the extent practicable, the use of advanced biofuels;

children.label: iii
children.text: demonstrate the greatest commitment on the part of the applicant to ensure funding for the proposed project and the greatest likelihood that the project will be maintained or expanded after Federal assistance under this subsection is completed;

children.label: iv
children.text: represent a partnership of public and private entities; and

children.label: v
children.text: exceed the minimum requirements of paragraph (3)(A)(ii).

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: A Maximum amount
children.text: The Secretary shall provide not more than $20,000,000 in Federal assistance under the pilot program to any applicant.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: B Cost sharing
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of any activity relating to renewable fuel blend infrastructure development carried out using funds from a grant under this subsection shall be not less than 20 percent.

children.heading: Maximum period of grants
children.label: C Maximum period of grants
children.text: The Secretary shall not provide funds to any applicant under the pilot program for more than 2 years.

children.heading: Deployment and distribution
children.label: D Deployment and distribution
children.text: The Secretary shall seek, to the maximum extent practicable, to ensure a broad geographic distribution of project sites funded by grants under this subsection.

children.heading: Transfer of information and knowledge
children.label: E Transfer of information and knowledge
children.text: The Secretary shall establish mechanisms to ensure that the information and knowledge gained by participants in the pilot program are transferred among the pilot program participants and to other interested parties, including other applicants that submitted applications.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register, Commerce Business Daily, and such other publications as the Secretary considers to be appropriate, a notice and request for applications to carry out projects under the pilot program.December 19, 2007

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: ii Deadline
children.text: An application described in clause (i) shall be submitted to the Secretary by not later than 180 days after the date of publication of the notice under that clause.

children.heading: Initial selection
children.label: iii Initial selection
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date by which applications for grants are due under clause (ii), the Secretary shall select by competitive, peer-reviewed proposal up to 5 applications for projects to be awarded a grant under the pilot program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register, Commerce Business Daily, and such other publications as the Secretary considers to be appropriate, a notice and request for additional applications to carry out projects under the pilot program that incorporate the information and knowledge obtained through the implementation of the first round of projects authorized under the pilot program.December 19, 2007

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: ii Deadline
children.text: An application described in clause (i) shall be submitted to the Secretary by not later than 180 days after the date of publication of the notice under that clause.

children.heading: Initial selection
children.label: iii Initial selection
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date by which applications for grants are due under clause (ii), the Secretary shall select by competitive, peer-reviewed proposal such additional applications for projects to be awarded a grant under the pilot program as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: an identification of the grant recipients and a description of the projects to be funded under the pilot program;

children.label: ii
children.text: an identification of other applicants that submitted applications for the pilot program but to which funding was not provided; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of the mechanisms used by the Secretary to ensure that the information and knowledge gained by participants in the pilot program are transferred among the pilot program participants and to other interested parties, including other applicants that submitted applications.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: B Evaluation
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and annually thereafter until the termination of the pilot program, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report containing an evaluation of the effectiveness of the pilot program, including an assessment of the petroleum displacement and benefits to the environment derived from the projects included in the pilot program.December 19, 2007

children.heading: Restriction
children.label: e Restriction
children.text: No grant shall be provided under subsection (b) or (c) to a large, vertically integrated oil company.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary for carrying out this section $200,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2008 through 2014.
